top_signals.title: Why India Will Stick with America
top_signals.summary:
The Trump administration has not been kind to India. Nevertheless, India has responded with far greater equanimity than most observers expected. Predictions that President Donald Trump’s behavior will hurt U.S.-Indian relations have not come to pass, with the leaders of the two countries remaining in frequent and friendly conversation. Instead of retaliating, India has responded calmly, though the Indian government faces considerable domestic criticism. Despite continuing irritation with American behavior, India can be expected to maintain its two-decade course of seeking good relations with the United States.The reason is a simple one that is ignored by critics and analysts: India

The post Why India Will Stick with America appeared first on War on the Rocks.

top_signals.summary: HM Treasury's statistics are governed by the standards set out by the UK Statistics Authority in their Code of Practice for accredited official statistics.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Suspected cyberattack triggers false emergency alerts across parts of Brazil
top_signals.summary: The incident occurred early Saturday when at least a dozen unauthorized alerts were sent through Brazil's Civil Defense Alert system, a platform designed to warn residents about imminent threats such as floods, landslides and other natural disasters.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Commission welcomes political agreement on criminal law rules to fight child sexual abuse
top_signals.summary: European Commission Press release Brussels, 22 Jun 2026 The Commission welcomes today's political agreement with the European Parliament and Council on the Directive updating the criminal law rules on child sexual abuse and sexual exploitation. Worrisome trends like the proliferation of artificial intelligence (AI)-generated abuse material make today's agreement even more timely.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Solana treasury firm Solmate’s largest stakeholder sues board for self-dealing, fiduciary breaches
top_signals.summary: Board members Ron Sade and Keren Maimon personally bought about 2.298 million SLMT shares at $4.97, diluting shareholders ~20%.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Strive says digital credit selloff was a liquidation event, not a credit crisis
Solar generation in CAISO surpassed natural gas in the first five months of 2026
top_signals.summary: In the first five months of 2026, utility-scale solar generation surpassed natural gas generation in CAISO. Solar electricity generation in the California Independent System Operator (CAISO) over the first five months of 2026 increased 21% compared with the same period in 2024, and natural gas generation decreased by 60%, data from our Hourly Electric Grid Monitor shows.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: A Glimpse into the “Search Your Target” Market for Stolen Credentials
top_signals.summary: Attackers no longer need to sift through massive credential dumps. They can pay others to do it for them. Flare explores how an emerging underground market searches stolen credential databases for specific companies, domains, and accounts. [...]
